# Drift-Aware Retraining Project - Final Report

Dataset profile: **ACS**

## 1) Experiment A: Static vs Adaptive System

## 2) Experiment B: Immediate vs Policy-Based Retraining
Immediate retraining heavily punishes system resources on every drift detected. Policy-based enforces cooldowns and sample accumulators.

## Core Research Conclusion
The best mean-F1 policy in this run was **Static** (mean_f1=0.6678, mean_accuracy=0.7327). **Policy-Standard** retrained 6 times versus 11 for **Immediate** and 0 for **Static**. Policy-based retraining did not beat the static baseline on mean F1 in this run; this suggests the simulated drift was detectable but not strong enough to justify every retrain under the current policy and perturbation design. Policy-Standard saw 5 concept-drift batches and achieved a mean next-batch F1 recovery gain of -0.0116 after retraining events.
